records.summary: A post-procedure KUB X-ray of a 46-year-old male showing the pelvicalyceal system with a urinary catheter in the bladder.
key_observations: A urinary catheter is visible, extending from the bladder to the perineum.
Spine and pelvic bones are clearly visualized.

records.stage_evidence: This is a radiology report summarizing findings from an IVP procedure.

ocr.verbatim:
NAME : MR. 46 YRS/M
REF. BY:

Report
DATE: FEB, 23, 2026

EXAMINATION PERFORMED: IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram)

*Study performed with non-ionic contrast media.

Plain X-Ray KUB:-
Large radio-opacity of size approx. 30 x 17.5 mm noted in the left paraspinal region at the level of L3/L4 intervertebral disc space.
The soft tissue outline are normal, both psoas shadows are well visualized.
No obvious mass lesion is seen.

IVP
50 cc of non-ionic contrast medium (Omnipaque) was injected without any untoward reaction and subsequently films were taken.

Left kidney not showing uptake and excretion of contrast even on delayed images.

The radio-opacity is noted in the plane film likely lies in the proximal ureter.
Right pelvicalyseal system appear normal.
Right ureter normal in course, caliber and pacification.
Urinary bladder is smooth in outline.
No evidence of filling defect or external pressure impression or diverticulum seen.

IMPRESSION:-
• Possibility of left proximal ureteric calculus.
• Left kidney not showing uptake and excretion of contrast even on delayed images.

Adv: NCCT KUB /USG correlation.

MD, DNB, MNAMS (RADIO DIAGNOSIS)
Ex-
24 Consultant Radiologist)

3 TESLA MRI
CT Scan
4D/3D Ultrasound
Colour Doppler
Digital X-ray

The report and films are not valid for medicolegal purposes.
This is only a professional opinion and not a diagnosis. It should be clinically correlated.
In case of any discrepancy due to machine or typing error please get it rectified immediately.
Measurements can vary from machine to machine.

यहाँ भूत लिंग का प्राक्षण नहीं होता है, यह दृष्टिनीय अपार्थ है

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for an IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram) performed on a 46-year-old male on February 23, 2026. The report details findings from both plain X-ray KUB and IVP studies, noting a large radio-opacity in the left paraspinal region and a lack of contrast uptake in the left kidney, suggesting a possible left proximal ureteric calculus.
key_observations: Plain X-ray KUB shows a large radio-opaque lesion (30 x 17.5 mm) in the left paraspinal region at L3/L4.
IVP findings indicate the left kidney is not showing contrast uptake or excretion, and a radio-opacity is likely in the proximal ureter.
Final impression suggests a possibility of left proximal ureteric calculus.
Recommendation for NCCT KUB or USG correlation.

records.stage_evidence: Sequential frames showing contrast progression through kidneys, ureters, and bladder
records.summary: A six-panel IVP (Intravenous Pyelogram) film showing the progression of contrast medium through the urinary tract over time, from a pre-contrast KUB to delayed views at 5 minutes, 15 minutes, 2.30 hours, and 16 hours post-injection.
key_observations: Six sequential radiographic views of the abdomen and pelvis
Labels indicate time points: KUB (pre-contrast), NEPH (nephrogram), 5MIN, 15MIN, 2.30H, 16H
Contrast medium is visible in the kidneys, ureters, and bladder at later time points
Patient is 46 years old, male, IVP performed on 23-02-2026
Maternity status indicated on the image
